filter_index.pickup_city: Sheikh Feroze Tours & Travels, Sathu, Barbara Shah Chowk, Srinagar

recommended_months: Second week of July to the beginning of September
avoid_months: Other seasons (outside July-September) due to cold and snow-covered trail and meadows
snow_or_rain_notes: Upper reaches under heavy snow until first week of July; lower meadows open earlier
Remnants of snow by Harnag and Harbhagwan lakes in July
No snow from end of August to first week of September
Kashmir receives only mild rainfall in July and August due to Pir Panjal range blocking rain clouds
temperature_notes: Mid-July to third week of August: Day 15-20°C, Night 4-8°C
End of August to first week of September: Day 12-18°C, Night 2-6°C; cold sets in as season progresses
End of September may not be the most comfortable time due to cold temperatures

experience_suitability: Suitable for fit beginners and experienced trekkers
terrain_challenges: Significant elevation changes throughout the trail
Challenging climb to Harnag Pass at 12,500 ft that is steep and rocky
Large boulders and uneven terrain near Harnag Pass increasing risk of slips and falls
Strenuous climbs and descents on the trail to Harbhagwan Lake at 13,789 ft
Long trekking days, some exceeding 10 kilometers
altitude_or_weather_risks: Altitude gain is quick, exceeding 11,000 ft within two trekking days
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) above 10,000 ft, affecting first-time and experienced trekkers alike
Even fit trekkers climbing too high too soon are at equal risk of AMS
Early-season trails can be wet and muddy after snowmelt
Streams flow at full force early in the season, making river crossings tricky

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 32 minutes
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date
Without fitness approval, trekkers will not be allowed on the trek
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the fitness criteria
Use apps like Nike and Strava to record runs
Paste the public profile link on the Indiahikes account
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
6-week fitness plan available for running and walking

experience_themes: Early July: remnants of snow by Harnag and Harbhagwan lakes, possible melting ice floes in early July

Mid-July to third week of August: bright green meadows carpeting the trail, wildflowers in Shul Margi and Bhaj Margi plains (violet, red, blue, purple, yellow, white), deep blue alpine lake waters, evening showers
End of August to first week of September: meadows turning golden brown, flowers wilting, brilliant blue skies when clouds part, deeper blue lake reflections, frequent evening showers, cold setting in
Rugged grey mountains rising at the fringes contrasting with green meadows
Alpine lakes Harnag and Harbhagwan with deep blue waters
Brilliant blue skies reflected in alpine lakes when clouds part
primary_watchouts: Upper reaches under heavy snow until first week of July
Evening showers become frequent from end of August
Cold sets in as season progresses to end of August
End of September may not be the most comfortable time due to cold temperatures
Trekking outside July-September window due to cold and snow-covered trail/meadows
End of September trekking due to cold temperatures (though doable)
best_for: Trekkers seeking comfortable monsoon-season trekking with mild rainfall
Trekkers wanting to see wildflower-dotted green meadows (mid-July to third week of August)
Trekkers interested in seeing snow remnants and frozen lake scenery (early July)
Trekkers wanting brilliant blue skies and golden-brown meadow tones (end of August to first week of September)
not_ideal_for: Trekking outside July-September window due to cold and snow-covered trail/meadows
End of September trekking due to cold temperatures (though doable)
